prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single small explorer walks one bright winding path across a wide foggy plain while dozens of faint ghostly translucent versions of the same explorer walk other untaken paths fanning out behind, the solid one wearing a tiny crown as if mistaking itself for the whole crowd. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and immediately readable. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: One solid cartoon explorer walks a single winding path while many faint translucent copies walk untaken paths fanning out around it, dramatizing how an observed time series is just one realization drawn from an entire ensemble of histories that never happened.
caption: Every time series you hold is one road actually walked out of a whole universe of roads that could have been; the trick of this chapter is reasoning about the crowd from the single traveler.

after_heading: 4. Ergodicity: When Time Averages Estimate Ensemble Averages
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single weathered traveler walking a very long looping road that coils through many seasons and moods, and as the road stretches on, a translucent crowd of varied parallel figures gradually merges and overlays onto the one traveler, as if one long life is quietly averaging out to equal a whole population at once. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A single traveler on a long coiling road slowly accumulates a translucent overlay of many different parallel figures, showing how ergodicity lets one path's time averages stand in for the ensemble average across all possible paths.
caption: Ergodicity is the quiet permission slip that lets one long life speak for an entire crowd of lives never lived: average over enough time on one path and you recover what averaging across all paths would have told you.

after_heading: 3. The Random Walk and Unit Roots
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: two cartoon wanderers on a flat plain with a faint horizontal center line, on the left a carefree wanderer drifting freely far off into the distance never returning toward the line, on the right a wanderer attached by a stretchy spring to a post on the center line, always tugged gently back toward it. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces and body language, one clear visual contrast, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Two cartoon wanderers near a faint center line: one drifts freely away forever with no pull back, the other is held by a stretchy spring to a post on the line, contrasting a unit-root random walk that never reverts with a stationary series tethered to its mean.
caption: A unit root is a wanderer with no spring: nothing pulls it back to a center, so it drifts forever, while a stationary series is gently tethered and always reverts toward its mean.

prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a small cartoon figure cups its hands and shouts across a canyon, and the echo returns as a row of progressively smaller, fainter copies of the same sound ripple shapes spaced at regular intervals receding into the distance, each one dimmer than the last. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face and body language, one clear visual metaphor, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cartoon figure shouts across a canyon and the echo returns as evenly spaced sound ripples that shrink and fade with distance, picturing the autocorrelation function as the regular, decaying echo of a series correlated with its own past at increasing lags.
caption: The autocorrelation function is the echo of a series talking to its own past: a shout that comes back at regular distances, fading as the lag grows, and the pattern of that fade is the fingerprint of what made the sound.

prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a round cheerful cartoon character shown in a circular strip of four seasonal scenes, puffing up large and bundled in scarf and snow during winter, then deflating slim under summer sun, then swelling again the next winter, the same swelling-and-shrinking repeating predictably around the ring of seasons. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive face and body language, one clear repeating visual gag, minimal clutter, generous negative space. alt: A round cartoon character cycles around four seasonal scenes, swelling up bundled in winter and shrinking under summer sun, then swelling again the next winter, personifying a seasonal component that peaks punctually at the same point every cycle.
caption: Seasonality is the character who swells punctually every winter and shrinks every summer: a deterministic surface pattern that repeats on the calendar, which you can strip off before modeling whatever wriggles underneath.

caption: Decomposition is patiently unbraiding one tangled series into three strands, a smooth trend, a repeating season, and a frizzy remainder, so each can be modeled or removed on its own terms.

after_heading: 4. Correlogram Diagnostics: Testing Residuals for Whiteness
prompt: A witty, cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a small innocent-looking cartoon residual character holding up its hands and shrugging as if to insist it is pure random static, while behind its back it trails a clearly visible neat repeating wave-shaped tail it is failing to hide, a magnifying glass hovering nearby catching the hidden regular pattern. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive guilty face and body language, one clear visual irony, minimal clutter, generous negative space. Suitable for a technical textbook. The humor is dry and knowing. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A guilty-looking cartoon residual shrugs and insists it is pure random noise while a neat repeating wave-shaped tail it failed to hide trails behind it, caught by a hovering magnifying glass, dramatizing a Ljung-Box test exposing leftover autocorrelation.
caption: A residual that swears it is nothing but noise but trails a tidy repeating tail is exactly what the Ljung-Box test is built to catch: leftover structure means your model still has work to do.
